title: Ed-ready Architecture Work
summary: Cloud-backed educational lab platform work focused on onboarding, stack comprehension, architecture clarity, documentation alignment, and security-conscious review of AWS/Pulumi deployment flows.

logs.title: Merged 10-day Ed-ready onboarding architecture package
logs.details: Added a structured onboarding package that maps the Ed-ready platform across Nuxt routing, stack responsibilities, frontend-to-backend flow, authentication, lab listing, launch flow, lab execution, backend session lifecycle, remote machine architecture, LTI, AWS, Pulumi, and production architecture.

logs.title: Opened documentation cleanup and accuracy audit
logs.details: Started a documentation-only cleanup pass to inventory the docs structure, compare documentation claims against the current codebase, update GitHub-readable indexes, clarify local setup around lab_platform, and separate current implementation reality from older architecture notes.

logs.title: Advanced documentation audit and lab session architecture mapping
logs.details: Reviewed top-level and main technical documentation, converted a generic Nuxt dependency note into repo-specific contributor guidance, added a current Mermaid-based lab session flow diagram, and marked older visual architecture material as partially accurate instead of treating it as source of truth.

logs.title: Paused cloud quick start rewrite pending maintainer direction
logs.details: Identified that the cloud quick start should wait for the related deployment flow review to settle before being rewritten. The next pass is intentionally approval-gated so the documentation reflects the accepted path rather than stale setup assumptions.

logs.title: Reviewed and hardened PR #43 AWS/Pulumi security flow
logs.details: Completed a focused security review and cleanup pass on PR #43 around the AWS/Pulumi lab lifecycle boundary. Work included fixing stale token-validation behavior, enforcing authentication before infrastructure side effects, reducing authorizer IAM blast radius, removing exposure of an incomplete AMI-capture route, making the machine-image allowlist fail closed, adjusting short authorizer caching so it works safely across protected lifecycle routes, removing raw request logging from lifecycle Lambdas, documenting a one-time migration caveat for legacy untagged instances, and opening follow-up issues for larger architecture decisions. Final automated review returned with no major issues; live AWS validation remains pending.

logs.title: Mapped Creator Mode and Save Version across AMI and container-backed labs
logs.details: Reviewed the proposed creator-mode lab versioning model against AWS ECS, ECR, EC2, and AMI behavior. The current working abstraction is Creator Mode, Modify, Save Version, Test, then Publish. EC2-backed labs map naturally to managed creator instances that can be saved into new AMI-backed LabEnvironmentVersion records. Container-backed labs should use a reproducible artifact path: edit source or workspace files, build a new immutable image, push it to ECR with a versioned tag or digest, register a new ECS task definition revision, then record and test that version before publishing. This clarified that live ECS task capture is likely not the clean source-of-truth model; the safer implementation is to treat container images as built artifacts rather than mutable running environments. The conclusion is still intentionally bounded: the abstraction looks sound, while the exact container creator workflow needs implementation design, cost review, and live AWS validation.

nextSteps: Wait for maintainer review and live AWS/Pulumi validation of PR #43
Validate the updated lab lifecycle deployment path once limited AWS access or maintainer-run smoke tests are available
Rewrite the cloud quick start only after PR #43 is accepted as the deployment/security source of truth
Continue documentation audit with non-blocked technical docs
Track larger hardening follow-ups separately: dedicated IAM roles per Lambda, proper AMI-capture workflow design, final authorizer cache strategy, and container-backed Save Version workflow design
Validate the practical MVP for container-backed labs: pull from ECR, edit/build locally or in a controlled workspace, push a versioned image, and register or update the ECS task definition revision
Convert approved architecture findings into recruiter/client-readable project notes
blockers: Live environment validation requires limited AWS access or maintainer-provided verification
Cloud quick start rewrite depends on maintainer approval of the updated deployment path
Older visual diagrams should be retained until maintainers confirm whether to replace, rebuild, or archive them
Container-backed lab versioning still needs a confirmed implementation path before it should be documented as final behavior
